basics.name: Tingyu Zhu
basics.summary: Ph.D. Candidate in Operations Research at UC Berkeley, with minors in math and machine learning.

work.summary: Designed and implemented a self-correcting AI Agent framework for structured data transformation. Optimized agent workflow documentation, reducing prompt and output volume by 70-80%.

work.company: Shanghai Aizeer Information Technology Co., Ltd.
work.summary: Developed real-time driving state recognition algorithm based on iPhone sensors. Developed generative AI model for controllable symbolic music generation.
Research on heavy-tailed conditional generation via diffusion models, constrained sequential experiments, and stochastic modeling via GANs.

education.area: Ph.D. in Operations Research (with minors in math and machine learning)
B.S. in Mathematics and Applied Mathematics

publications.name: ResiliBench: Evaluating Agentic Workflow Adaptation in Stochastic Environments
publications.summary: R. Ao†, Z. Min†, T. Zhu†, W. Yin, X. Wang (co-first author)

publications.name: Efficient Fine-Grained Guidance for Diffusion-Based Symbolic Music Generation
publications.summary: T. Zhu†, H. Liu†, Z. Wang, Z. Jiang, Z. Zheng (co-first author)

publications.name: A Doubly Stochastic Simulator with Applications in Arrivals Modeling and Simulation

publications.name: Learning to Simulate Sequentially Generated Data via Neural Networks and Wasserstein Training
